expectations.text: Classifies the system as high-risk via the Art. 6(1) path: safety component of a product covered by Annex I Union harmonisation legislation (MDR) that undergoes third-party conformity assessment
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'this is a high-risk AI system under Art. 6(1), Path A — the Annex I "embedded product safety component" route' and lists both limbs: safety component of Annex I MDR-covered product, and MDR requires notified-body assessment.

expectations.text: States the compliance deadline for Annex I embedded high-risk systems as 2 August 2028 (as amended by the Digital Omnibus; originally 2 August 2027). Any deadline stated must be Aug 2028.
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'the Digital Omnibus (adopted 29 June 2026) confirmed as: 2 August 2028... This is later than the Annex III standalone-system deadline of 2 December 2027.'

expectations.text: Explains the integrated conformity route: AI Act requirements are assessed within the existing MDR notified-body procedure (Art. 43(3)) rather than a separate parallel assessment, with a single set of technical documentation permitted
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Conformity assessment is integrated into the existing MDR procedure. Rather than running a separate AI Act conformity assessment process, the AI Act requirements (Arts. 9–15) are assessed as part of your existing MDR notified-body conformity assessment... you do not go through two entirely separate certification tracks.'

expectations.text: Identifies the dual-regulator reality: notified body under MDR plus AI Act requirements (Arts. 9-15) folded into the MDR QMS/technical documentation
expectations.evidence: Response states MDR notified body 'needs to be designated/qualified to also assess AI Act requirements' and describes QMS integration: 'Your existing MDR QMS... can be adapted and integrated to cover the AI Act's 13 QMS components' with a table mapping Arts. 9-17 to 'MDR Integration Point'.

expectations.text: Gives practical sequencing advice (align AI Act risk management with ISO 14971 processes, update technical file, involve the notified body early)
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Your MDR risk management file (ISO 14971) should be extended to explicitly cover the AI Act's 5-step process' and 'Talk to your MDR notified body now about their AI Act assessment capability/designation' and 'Immediate action: Engage your MDR notified body on AI Act assessment scope, and gap-map your existing MDR risk/QMS/PMS documentation against Arts. 9–17.'
